content.cn_text: 第四部分。你将听到一段关于纳米技术及其应用的讲座。首先，
content.en_text: Part 4. You will hear part of a lecture on nanotechnology and its applications. First,

content.cn_text: 你有一些时间查看第31至40题。
content.en_text: you have some time to look at questions 31 to 40.

content.cn_text: 现在请仔细听，并回答第31至40题。
content.en_text: Now, listen carefully and answer questions 31 to 40.

content.cn_text: 今天的讲座是纳米技术系列讲座的第一讲。纳米技术
content.en_text: Today's lecture is the first of a series of lectures on nanotechnology. Nanotechnology

content.cn_text: 有时简称为 nanotech，指的是在原子和
content.en_text: sometimes shortened to nanotech refers to the manipulation of matter on an atomic and

content.cn_text: 分子层面对物质进行操控。它是在纳米尺度上进行的科学、工程和技术，
content.en_text: molecular level. It's a science, engineering and technology conducted at the nanoscale,

content.cn_text: 这个尺度大约是1到100纳米。它是对极小事物的研究和应用，
content.en_text: which is about 1 to 100 nanometers. It is a study and application of extremely small

content.cn_text: 并且可以用于所有其他科学领域。它到底有多小呢？一纳米
content.en_text: things and can be used across all the other science fields. Just how small is that? A nanometer

content.cn_text: 是十亿分之一米。纳米尺度研究的想法最早来自一位物理学家，
content.en_text: is 1 billionth of a meter. The idea of nanoscale research first started from a physicist

content.cn_text: 名叫理查德·费曼，时间是1959年。他设想了一种过程，在这个过程中科学家能够
content.en_text: called Richard Feynman in 1959. He envisaged a process in which scientists would be able

content.cn_text: 操控并控制单个原子和分子。事实上，直到1981年，
content.en_text: to manipulate and control individual atoms and molecules. In fact, it wasn't until 1981

content.cn_text: 随着一种特殊显微镜的发明，现代纳米技术才真正开始。
content.en_text: with the invention of a special kind of microscope that modern nanotechnology truly began.

content.cn_text: 尽管纳米技术已经创造出许多新材料和新设备，并拥有广泛的
content.en_text: Even though nanotechnology has created many new materials and devices with a vast range

content.cn_text: 应用，但人们始终担心这些非天然的产品。许多人
content.en_text: of applications, there is always concern over these unnatural products. Many people are

content.cn_text: 担心它们可能对环境、健康和安全构成威胁，也就是
content.en_text: worried that they may pose a threat to the environment, health and safety also known

content.cn_text: EHS。为了更好地理解和评估潜在危害，人们引入了风险评估。
content.en_text: as EHS. In order to better understand and evaluate the potential harm, risk assessment has

content.cn_text: 目前也有关于纳米 EHS 研究及相关
content.en_text: been introduced. There are also ongoing international collaborations on a nanoeHS research and related

content.cn_text: 政策问题的持续国际合作。尽管存在担忧，纳米技术仍以意想不到的方式使社会受益。在
content.en_text: policy issues. Despite the concern nanotechnology has benefited society in unexpected ways, in

content.cn_text: 食品加工行业中，纳米技术被用来应对铁缺乏，
content.en_text: the food processing industry, nanotechnology is leveraged against iron deficiency, which

content.cn_text: 铁缺乏会引发贫血。例如，通过减小土豆中的颗粒大小，这项新技术
content.en_text: triggers anemia. For example, by reducing the particle size in potatoes, this new technology

content.cn_text: 提高了这种营养成分的生物利用度，同时不会容易导致颜色
content.en_text: improves the bioavailability of this nutritional ingredient without the tendency to cause color

content.cn_text: 和气味发生变化。在包装方面，纳米材料更经济，并被用于
content.en_text: and odor changes. In terms of packaging, nanomaterials are more economical and are used

content.cn_text: 各种产品中，尤其是巧克力。纳米技术还可以增强食物的风味，
content.en_text: in all sorts of products, chocolate in particular. Nanotechnology can also enhance the flavor

content.cn_text: 在家庭层面，我们预计会制造更多智能家具，这些家具由形状记忆
content.en_text: of food on a domestic scale we expect more smart furniture, which is made of shape memory

content.cn_text: 合金制成。但纳米技术的应用远不止于此，
content.en_text: alloys to be manufactured. But nanotechnology has much wider application

content.cn_text: 它还可以帮助发展可持续农业。它在
content.en_text: than this and could also help develop sustainable agriculture. It offers great potential to

content.cn_text: 增强肥料效果方面具有巨大潜力，从而最大限度减少环境影响，并
content.en_text: strengthen the effect of fertilizers, thereby minimizing the environmental impact and

content.cn_text: 提高土壤中植物的生产力。在医学领域，生物工程师
content.en_text: boosting the productivity of plants in the soil. In the area of medicine, bioengineers

content.cn_text: 已经开发出简单且廉价的纳米级递送载体。像碳
content.en_text: have developed simple and inexpensive nanoscale delivery vehicles. Conductors like carbon

content.cn_text: 纳米管这样的导体已经被发明出来，具有不同的结构。这类纳米管变得越来越
content.en_text: nanotubes have been invented with varying structures. Such nanotubes have become increasingly

content.cn_text: 吸引研究人员，因为它们能够高效地将药物送入静脉。
content.en_text: attractive to researchers because of their ability to efficiently deliver drugs into veins.

content.cn_text: 这意味着装载在其上的更高剂量药物可以从给药
content.en_text: This means that higher doses of drugs loaded on them can be transported from the administration

content.cn_text: 部位运输到作用相关部位，比如癌症病灶，从而减少有毒副作用，
content.en_text: sites to the effects-related sites, such as cancer focuses, thus decreasing toxic side

content.cn_text: 同时提高治疗效果。研究团队还开发了一种被广泛
content.en_text: effects while increasing the treatment effects. The research team has also developed a widely

content.cn_text: 接受的材料，可用于涂覆不同表面，包括木材、塑料
content.en_text: accepted material that can be used to coat different surfaces, including wood, plastic

content.cn_text: 和玻璃。它主要由银组成。这类纳米颗粒最终可以杀死一些
content.en_text: and glass. It is mainly comprised of silver. Such nanoparticles can eventually kill some

content.cn_text: 微生物，因为它具有抗菌特性。整个过程简单、易于应用，
content.en_text: microbes due to the antiseptic properties. The whole process is simple, easy to apply

content.cn_text: 更重要的是对环境无害。令人惊讶的是，纳米技术对我们的健康也有重大影响。
content.en_text: and more importantly harmless to the environment. Surprisingly, nanotechnology has major implications

content.cn_text: 通过一种特定类型的蛋白质改变代谢率，
content.en_text: for our health as well. By changing the metabolic rate through a particular type of protein,

content.cn_text: 肥胖患者能够更快燃烧卡路里，即使在高热量饮食下也是如此。因此，
content.en_text: obese patients are able to burn calories faster, even under a high calorie diet. Therefore,

content.cn_text: 治疗项目被提供给患者，通过处理多余脂肪来减轻体重，
content.en_text: treatment programs are provided for patients to lose weight by disposing of excess fat

content.cn_text: 并且这种方式相对更安全。这一发现可能有助于解释为什么超重人群很难
content.en_text: in a relatively safer manner. The finding may help explain why overweight people struggle

content.cn_text: 减重。他们储存的脂肪会在分子层面主动抵抗他们燃烧脂肪的努力。
content.en_text: to lose weight. Their stored fat is actively fighting against their efforts to burn it off

content.cn_text: 进一步研究主要集中在临床和商业
content.en_text: at the molecular level. Further research is mainly focused on the clinical and commercial

content.cn_text: 治疗的发展上，涉及肥胖、糖尿病以及其他相关疾病，例如心脏
content.en_text: developments of therapies for obesity, diabetes and other associated conditions such as heart

content.cn_text: 疾病。在化妆品方面，纳米技术和纳米材料的应用可以在
content.en_text: disease. In terms of cosmetics, the applications of nanotechnology and nanomaterials can be found

content.cn_text: 许多产品中看到。纳米技术在化妆品中有两个主要用途。第一个
content.en_text: in many products. There are two main uses for nanotechnology in cosmetics. The first

content.cn_text: 是纳米颗粒通常被用作抗衰老防晒产品中的紫外线过滤剂，
content.en_text: one is that nanoparticles are commonly used as UV filters in anti-aging sun care products

content.cn_text: 以避免由太阳造成的皮肤损伤。此外，纳米技术在
content.en_text: to avoid skin damage caused by the sun. In addition, nanotechnology has played an important

content.cn_text: 向皮肤输送活性成分方面也发挥了重要作用。这种机制被证明能提高
content.en_text: role in delivering active ingredients to the skin. The mechanism proves to improve the

content.cn_text: 活性成分的生物利用度，并增强皮肤保湿。纳米技术种类繁多的
content.en_text: bioavailability of active and enhanced skin hydration. The wide variety of nanotechnology

content.cn_text: 应用毫无疑问表明，这一领域只会继续变得更加令人兴奋，
content.en_text: applications leave no doubt that this field will only continue to get more exciting and

content.cn_text: 我期待看到这些发展进一步拓展。
content.en_text: I look forward to seeing these developments further branch out.

content.cn_text: 第四部分到此结束。现在你有一分钟时间检查第四部分的答案。
content.en_text: That is the end of Part 4. You now have one minute to check your answers to Part 4.

groups.desc:
Complete the notes below.
Write ONE WORD ONLY for each answer.
groups.title: Questions 31–40

items.title: The development in nanotechnology is the result of a new type of 31. __________.
items.analysis: 答案解析：第31题问纳米技术的发展源于一种新型的什么，原文说 with the invention of a special kind of microscope，因此答案是 microscope。

items.title: People are worried that nanotech products are 32. __________.
items.analysis: 答案解析：第32题问人们担心纳米技术产品是怎样的，原文说 concern over these unnatural products，因此答案是 unnatural。

items.title: The availability of 33. __________ in our diet (e.g. in potatoes) is greatly improved.
items.analysis: 答案解析：第33题问饮食中哪种成分的可利用性被大大提高，原文先说 iron deficiency，后面说 improves the bioavailability of this nutritional ingredient，因此答案是 iron。

items.title: The packaging cost, particularly 34. __________, is reduced.
items.analysis: 答案解析：第34题问包装成本特别是哪种产品被降低，原文说 chocolate in particular，因此答案是 chocolate。第35题问纳米技术能增加食物的什么，原文说 enhance the flavor，因此答案是 flavor/flavour。

items.title: Nanotechnology can increase the 35. __________ of food.
items.analysis: 答案解析：第34题问包装成本特别是哪种产品被降低，原文说 chocolate in particular，因此答案是 chocolate。第35题问纳米技术能增加食物的什么，原文说 enhance the flavor，因此答案是 flavor/flavour。

items.title: The efficiency of 36. __________ is increased.
items.analysis: 答案解析：第36题问哪一类东西的效率被提高，原文说 strengthen the effect of fertilizers，因此答案是 fertilizers/fertilisers。

items.title: Nanotubes allow more 37. __________ to enter veins.
items.analysis: 答案解析：第37题问纳米管能让更多什么进入静脉，原文说 deliver drugs into veins，因此答案是 drugs。

items.title: As the most popular material, 38. __________ is used to kill bacteria.
items.analysis: 答案解析：第38题问最常用来杀菌的材料是什么，原文说 mainly comprised of silver，并接着说 nanoparticles can kill some microbes，因此答案是 silver。

items.title: Nanotechnology is also helpful in 39. __________ loss program.
items.analysis: 答案解析：第39题问纳米技术有助于什么 loss program，原文说 programs are provided for patients to lose weight，因此答案是 weight。

items.title: Nanoparticles protect skin from the 40. __________ and help to improve the ability against aging.
items.analysis: 答案解析：第40题问纳米颗粒保护皮肤免受什么伤害，原文说 skin damage caused by the sun，因此答案是 sun。
